text: We propose a novel transformer architecture for protein folding prediction that achieves state-of-the-art results on the CASP14 benchmark. Our model combines attention mechanisms with geometric deep learning to capture spatial relationships between amino acid residues.
title: Deep Learning Approaches to Protein Structure Prediction

text: This paper examines the impact of monetary policy on income inequality across OECD countries from 2000 to 2020. Using panel data regression with fixed effects, we find that expansionary monetary policy disproportionately benefits asset holders.
title: Monetary Policy and Income Inequality: A Cross-Country Analysis

text: We present a formal proof of the Riemann hypothesis for function fields over finite fields using algebraic geometry techniques. Our approach generalizes the Weil conjectures and provides new insights into the distribution of prime numbers.
title: On the Riemann Hypothesis for Function Fields

text: This study investigates the neural correlates of decision-making under uncertainty using functional MRI. Participants performed a gambling task while brain activity was recorded, revealing activation patterns in the prefrontal cortex and anterior insula.
title: Neural Mechanisms of Decision-Making Under Uncertainty

text: We analyze the legal implications of autonomous vehicle accidents under current tort law frameworks. As self-driving technology advances, traditional notions of negligence and product liability require significant re-examination.
title: Autonomous Vehicles and Tort Liability: A Legal Framework

text: Using CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing, we developed a novel therapeutic approach for sickle cell disease. Our clinical trial results show that targeted modification of the BCL11A gene restores fetal hemoglobin production in 87% of patients.
title: CRISPR-Based Gene Therapy for Sickle Cell Disease

text: This paper traces the philosophical roots of environmental ethics from Aldo Leopold's land ethic through deep ecology to contemporary eco-feminism. We argue that non-anthropocentric frameworks offer more robust foundations for environmental policy.
title: Philosophical Foundations of Environmental Ethics

text: We design a quantum error correction code that achieves fault-tolerant computation with a threshold error rate of 1.1%. Our surface code implementation uses superconducting qubits arranged in a two-dimensional lattice topology.
title: Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computing with Surface Codes

text: This interdisciplinary study combines natural language processing with epidemiological modeling to predict disease outbreaks from social media data. Our system monitors Twitter posts in real-time and detected the 2023 flu season onset two weeks before traditional surveillance.
title: Social Media Mining for Early Disease Outbreak Detection

text: We present a novel composite material for bridge construction that combines carbon fiber reinforcement with self-healing concrete. Structural analysis shows a 40% increase in load-bearing capacity while reducing maintenance costs by 60% over a 50-year lifecycle.
title: Self-Healing Composite Materials for Civil Infrastructure
